source_paper_title: Higher-Order Expansion and Bartlett Correctability of Distributionally Robust Optimization
context:
Section 8 ("Generalization to Multidimensional Settings"), page 16; also mentioned as a limitation/future work in the Introduction, page 3.

On the other hand, a current limitation of the DRO formulation (1) is that it is single-dimensional, whereas EL can generate multi-dimensional confidence regions. We leave the investigation of auxiliary information incorporation and multi-dimensional generalization to the future, but provide some hints to the latter extension in Section 8.
title: Higher-order Bartlett-type correction for multidimensional divergence-based DRO confidence regions
latex:
Given i.i.d. data \(X_1,\dots,X_n\) drawn from an unknown distribution \(P_0\) on a measurable space \((\mathcal{X},\mathcal{F})\), let \(\hat P_n:=\frac1n\sum_{i=1}^n\delta_{X_i}\) be the empirical distribution. Fix a convex function \(\varphi:(0,\infty)\to\mathbb{R}\) with \(\varphi(1)=\varphi'(1)=0\) and define the \(\varphi\)-divergence
\[
D_{\varphi}(P\|Q):=\int \varphi\!\left(\frac{\mathrm{d}P}{\mathrm{d}Q}\right)\,\mathrm{d}Q,
\]
for probability measures \(P\) absolutely continuous with respect to \(Q\). Let \(\psi\) be a (nonparametric) von Mises differentiable functional mapping distributions on \((\mathcal{X},\mathcal{F})\) into \(\mathbb{R}^d\) for some \(d\ge 2\).

For a radius \(\eta>0\), define the divergence-based DRO image set (a candidate confidence region)
\[
A_{n,\eta}:=\{\psi(P):\; P\ \text{is a probability measure on }(\mathcal{X},\mathcal{F}),\ D_{\varphi}(P\|\hat P_n)\le \eta\}\subseteq\mathbb{R}^d.
\]

The problem is to design an explicit data-dependent calibration \(\eta=\eta(n,\alpha,\hat P_n)\) (e.g., a Bartlett-type correction to the usual \(\eta\asymp n^{-1}\) choice) and to prove, under verifiable regularity conditions on \(\varphi\) and \(\psi\), the higher-order coverage guarantee
\[
\mathbb{P}_{P_0}\big(\psi(P_0)\in A_{n,\eta(n,\alpha,\hat P_n)}\big)=1-\alpha+O(n^{-3/2})\quad\text{as }n\to\infty.
\]
Equivalently, characterize (and compute/estimate) the correction terms needed so that the multidimensional region \(A_{n,\eta}\) attains \(O(n^{-3/2})\) coverage error, extending the paper’s one-dimensional Bartlett-type correction for \(\psi(P_0)\in\mathbb{R}\).
